description: Rijksmuseum golden search cases (10 hand-authored draft cases, D9): the per-museum accuracy north-star for the Rijksmuseum rows in the merged artifact. Authored 2026-07-06 against LIVE data.rijksmuseum.nl lookups (objectNumber= search + id.rijksmuseum.nl Linked Art resolution) for well-known icons, verified on-view (current_location present) at authoring time. Titles are Dutch (title = source language; translateFrom: "nl" in the registry) — the DeepSeek translate.ts pass has NOT been run yet for this museum (pipeline-only, costs money, not part of this smoke validation), so titleAlt is empty and expectTitleContains below targets the DUTCH title actually indexed. Tiers mirror the Met set: autocomplete = local FTS top-8; full = all-results; llm = relaxed-FTS stand-in for the interpret tier. IMPORTANT: these 5 icons are sequential-id-sampled by the OAI harvest, not popularity-sampled, so none are expected to land in the --limit 100/400 smoke snapshots (~8-9k on-view objects total, smoke covers <5% of them) — every case below is marked whether it needs the full harvest to pass.

cases.note: NEEDS FULL HARVEST. Rembrandt's The Night Watch, SK-C-5 (id.rijksmuseum.nl/200107928); title 'De Nachtwacht'; on view HG-2.31 'Night Watch Gallery'/'Nachtwachtzaal', its own dedicated room. Verified live 2026-07-06.
NEEDS FULL HARVEST. Vermeer's The Milkmaid, SK-A-2344 (id.rijksmuseum.nl/200108369); title 'Het melkmeisje'; on view HG-2.30 (Gallery of Honour), case 3. Verified live 2026-07-06.

NEEDS FULL HARVEST. Vermeer's The Little Street, SK-A-2860 (id.rijksmuseum.nl/200108371); title 'Gezicht op huizen in Delft, bekend als ‘Het straatje’'; on view HG-2.30 (Gallery of Honour). Verified live 2026-07-06.

NEEDS FULL HARVEST. Vermeer's The Love Letter, SK-A-1595 (id.rijksmuseum.nl/200108370); title 'De liefdesbrief'; on view HG-2.30 (Gallery of Honour) — three Vermeers share this one room. Verified live 2026-07-06.

NEEDS FULL HARVEST. Rembrandt's Self-Portrait as the Apostle Paul, SK-A-4050 (id.rijksmuseum.nl/200107952); on view HG-2.30, case 7. Confirmed on view live 2026-07-06 — a second Rembrandt self-portrait (SK-A-4691, plain 'Zelfportret') was checked and is currently OFF view (no current_location), correctly excluded from the on-view snapshot.

NEEDS FULL HARVEST. Artist + title tokens; 'schutters' (militia) is genre vocabulary the Dutch classification carries for civic-guard group portraits, exercising the strict-AND tier across artist+classification+title.
NEEDS FULL HARVEST. Artist + title + Dutch classification token ('schilderij' = painting) — strict AND across three columns, same shape as the V&A/AIC full-tier cases.

cases.query: a large 17th century group portrait of an amsterdam civic militia company stepping out under a captain's command
cases.note: NEEDS FULL HARVEST. Descriptive phrasing for The Night Watch; 'militia'/'company'/'captain' are the surviving anchor tokens after stopword relaxation (title itself is Dutch, not searchable by english paraphrase without the real Gemini interpret tier — same documented gap as SMK's 1 relaxed-FTS miss).

cases.query: a dutch maid pouring milk from a jug in a kitchen
cases.note: NEEDS FULL HARVEST. Descriptive phrasing for The Milkmaid; same Dutch-title-vs-English-paraphrase gap as above.

cases.query: a narrow street between brick houses in a dutch town
cases.note: NEEDS FULL HARVEST. Descriptive phrasing for The Little Street; 'straatje' itself means 'little street' so this is closer to a translation than a paraphrase, a softer case than the other two llm-tier entries.
